mcqs.question: What is the primary role of Nimbus in Apache Storm architecture?

options.text: To execute spouts and bolts
To distribute code, assign tasks, and monitor topology health
To coordinate communication between components
To store streaming data

mcqs.explanation: Nimbus is the master node in Storm that distributes code across the cluster, assigns tasks to supervisors, monitors topology health, and handles failures. It's similar to Hadoop's JobTracker.

mcqs.question: In Apache Storm, what is a Spout?

options.text: A processing unit that transforms tuples
A data source that reads from external systems and emits tuples
A coordination service for the cluster
A database for storing streaming results

mcqs.explanation: A Spout is the source of streams in Storm—it reads data from external sources (Kafka, databases, APIs) and emits tuples into the topology. Spouts are the entry points for data.

mcqs.question: Which stream grouping in Storm ensures that tuples with the same field value always go to the same bolt task?

options.text: Shuffle Grouping
All Grouping
Fields Grouping
Global Grouping

mcqs.explanation: Fields Grouping routes tuples to bolt tasks based on specified field values, ensuring tuples with the same field value always go to the same task. This is essential for aggregations and stateful operations.

mcqs.question: How does Storm guarantee message processing?

options.text: By storing all tuples in a database
By tracking tuple lineage and using ack/fail mechanism with timeout
By processing each tuple exactly once
By discarding failed tuples

mcqs.explanation: Storm tracks the tuple tree lineage and uses ack (successful) and fail (unsuccessful) callbacks. If a tuple isn't acked within timeout (default 30s), it's replayed, providing at-least-once processing guarantee.

mcqs.question: An IoT application monitors 10,000 sensors sending data every second. Requirements: (1) Detect temperature anomalies within 100ms, (2) Aggregate hourly statistics per sensor, (3) Generate instant alerts. Which architecture is most appropriate?

options.text: Use Hadoop MapReduce for both real-time detection and aggregation
Use Storm with Fields Grouping for per-sensor aggregation, shuffle for detection, and alert bolts for instant notifications
Use Spark Streaming with 1-hour micro-batches
Store all data first, then batch process every hour

mcqs.explanation: Storm provides true streaming with sub-second latency needed for 100ms detection. Fields Grouping routes all tuples from same sensor to same bolt task enabling per-sensor stateful aggregation. Shuffle Grouping distributes detection load, and alert bolts provide instant notifications. MapReduce (A) has minute-level latency, Spark (C) batches delay alerts, and option D doesn't meet real-time requirements.
